title: Writing: Command the Streets
learningGoal: Construct sentences using the formal imperative mood to give directions, ask for prices, and negotiate.
instructions: You are navigating India. Translate the following phrases into polite Hindi commands. Use 'Aap' level commands (ending in 'iye') to ensure respect.
writingPrompt:
Translate the following sentences into Hindi using FORMAL commands:
1. Brother, please take a left from ahead. (Brother = Bhaiya, Ahead from = Aage se, Left = Baayein, Take = Lijiye/Leejiye)
2. Please go straight, then please stop here. (Straight = Seedha, Go = Chaliye, Then = Phir, Here = Yahaan, Stop = Rokiye)
3. Excuse me, how much is this for? (Excuse me = Suniye, This = Yeh, How much for = Kitne ka hai)
4. Please reduce a little. (A little = Thoda, Reduce = Kam, Do = Keejiye)
5. Please don't go there. (There = Wahaan, Do not = Mat, Go = Jaiye)
promptGoal: Apply formal imperatives and negative commands accurately.

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: भैया, आगे से बाएँ लीजिए। सीधा चलिए, फिर यहाँ रोकिए। सुनिए, यह कितने का है? थोड़ा कम कीजिए। वहाँ मत जाइए।
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: Bhaiya, aage se baayein leejiye. Seedha chaliye, phir yahaan rokiye. Suniye, yeh kitne ka hai? Thoda kam keejiye. Wahaan mat jaiye.
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: Brother, please take a left from ahead. Please go straight, then please stop here. Excuse me, how much is this for? Please reduce a little. Please do not go there.
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect application of the formal 'iye' endings. Correctly used 'mat' for the negative command.

evaluationCriteria.description: Must use 'leejiye', 'chaliye', 'rokiye', 'suniye', 'keejiye', 'jaiye'. Deduct heavily if informal roots (ruk, chal) are used.
Must use 'mat' in sentence 5. If 'nahi' is used, deduct points.
Must correctly use 'Baayein' (left), 'Seedha' (straight), and 'Kitne ka hai'.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: भैया, आगे से बाएँ लीजिए। सीधा चलिए, फिर यहाँ रोकिए। सुनिए, यह कितने का है? थोड़ा कम कीजिए। वहाँ मत जाइए।
acceptableAlternates: Bhaiya, aage se baayein leejiye. Seedha chaliye, phir yahaan rokiye. Suniye, yeh kitne ka hai? Thoda kam keejiye. Wahaan mat jaiye.
bhaiya aage se baayein leejiye. seedha chaliye phir yahaan rokiye. suniye yeh kitne ka hai. thoda kam keejiye. wahaan mat jaiye.
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Accepts transliteration. Strict check on 'iye' suffixes and 'mat'.

criteria.name: Nahi Error
criteria.description: If user writes 'Wahaan nahi jaiye', score drops by 30%.

feedbackRules.onPass: Outstanding! You are ready to confidently and politely navigate India.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the lesson. Formal commands ALWAYS end in 'iye' (Rokiye, Chaliye). Never use 'Nahi' when telling someone not to do something—use 'Mat'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try. Pay close attention to the irregular verbs. 'Do' becomes 'Keejiye', not 'Kariye'. 'Take' becomes 'Leejiye', not 'Liye'.

hintText: Sentence 4 uses the verb 'Karna' (To do). The formal command for Karna is highly irregular: Keejiye.
commonMistakes: Translating 'Excuse me' literally as 'Maaf kijiye' (Forgive me) instead of using 'Suniye' (Listen).
